title: Unknown short flag causes a panic instead of a clean error
body: Running `pkgx -z` hits `_ => panic!("unknown argument: -{}", c)` in parse() and dumps a full Rust backtrace to the terminal. This should print a normal 'unknown flag' usage error like the long-flag path does for unrecognized `--foo`.

title: Version symlinks (v*, vX.Y) are never created on Windows
body: symlink() in install.rs is gated behind #[cfg(not(windows))], so after installing a package on Windows there's no v*, v<major>, or v<major>.<minor> convenience symlink in the shelf directory like there is on macOS/Linux. Resolution against 'latest' installed version seems to rely on these existing.

title: Windows install lockfile is truncated before it's locked
body: On Windows, install() opens the lockfile with `.truncate(true)` every single time before calling lock_exclusive(), whereas the Unix path just opens the existing directory read-only. If two pkgx processes race here the truncate could clobber lock state right as another process is waiting on it.

title: Document PKGX_PANTRY_DIR override behavior in sync
body: sync::update() and sync::ensure() branch on whether PKGX_PANTRY_DIR is set and whether config.pantry_dir already has a projects/ directory, but none of this is explained anywhere for people running against a local pantry checkout during development. A comment or doc page would save a lot of source-diving.
